summary: mcp-server-pg postinstall harvests developer identity and cloud/SCM context to hardcoded remote endpoint
details: On `npm install`, the declared postinstall script `scripts/postinstall.js` runs automatically and collects installer-owned identity and configuration data from the developer's machine, then POSTs it to a hardcoded remote endpoint at npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. Collected fields include: OS hostname, OS username, current working directory, git-configured email parsed from ~/.gitconfig and ~/.config/git/config, git remote origin URL, up to 15 committer emails scraped from.git/logs/HEAD, SSH public-key comment emails read by enumerating ~/.ssh/ and parsing every *.pub file, GitHub CLI user/email from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, GCP project and account email from ~/.config/gcloud/properties, AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config, /etc/resolv.conf search domain, and parent project metadata. Reading from classic installer-secret directories (~/.ssh, ~/.aws, ~/.config/gcloud) and shipping the derived identifiers to an author-controlled endpoint at install time is credential-adjacent exfiltration — the exact developer-to-cloud-account mapping used for downstream targeting, regardless of the presence of a keyword filter on the transmitted lines or an opt-out environment variable.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
